meaning: adj.生理节奏的,以24小时为周期的
examples: Such a rhythm whose period is approximately—but not exactly—a day is called circadian.
Not everyone has an identical circadian rhythm.
This is a benign variation within circadian rhythms known as a chronotype.
Many of the body's processes are governed by circadian rhythms which repeat in 24-hour cycles.
Nodding off after dinner or wanting to sleep till noon could indicate circadian rhythm disorders.
